summary: Sprint 5 (Redesigned Data Management / Inventory) backend tested end-to-end: 28/28 pytest cases passed. Coverage: products CRUD with UPPERCASE-normalisation, duplicate 400, rename-cascade across inward+outward, dup-rename 400, delete-409 when references exist; inward POST/PATCH/DELETE with new fields (unit, bill_number, attachment_file_id/filename) + 404s; outward POST with project/challan/status, ?status=Pending filter, Pending→Dispatched transition, 404s; stats shape + low_stock using per-product min_stock + Pending outward NOT subtracted from balance (verified before & after dispatch confirmation); defaults GET + deep-merge PATCH preserving prior section data; history filters (type/product/vendor/client/search) + desc sort; history.csv 200 with text/csv + correct headers + data row; material-request approval auto-creates outward with status='Pending', source='auto-material-request', outward_challan_no, project_id/project_name and PATCH to Dispatched works; multi-tenant isolation across products/inward/outward/defaults/history (secondary tenant registered live); Sprint 2 ai-extract → bulk-inward end-to-end still works and entries surface in /inventory/history with source='ai-bulk-import'. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1902 lines — Sprint 5 added ~300 lines on top of the already-large file. Strongly recommend splitting Inventory endpoints into backend/routes/inventory.py (products, inward, outward, defaults, history) and a separate backend/routes/material_requests.py. Single-file approach is past its limit.

inv_history is fully Python-side filtered (acknowledged in spec). At >5k inward+outward rows per tenant the round-trip + filter loop will get slow. Consider a $match-based aggregation pipeline or compound indexes on (company_id, date) + (company_id, product, date).

inv_history_csv calls inv_history(user=user, ...) as a normal coroutine — works but bypasses FastAPI's Depends/Query validation. Acceptable but worth a comment / a thin internal helper to avoid the lint smell.

ensure_product backfills size/category/unit but never backfills min_stock. If a product was implicitly created by an early inward (min_stock=0) and admin later changes name/min_stock via UI, the cascade is fine — but min_stock can remain 0 for legacy products created before Sprint 5, silently flagging them Low Stock in /inventory/stats. Consider a one-time migration or surface a UI nudge.
delete_product 409 message says 'transactions reference this product' but combines inward+out into one count. UX-wise it might help to split: 'N inward, M outward'.
approve_material loops over items and inserts outward entries one-by-one (await inside a for-loop). For requests with many items this is 1 round-trip per item; use insert_many for atomicity & speed.

PATCH /api/inventory/inward and /outward require a full body matching InwardIn/OutwardIn — caller MUST resend product+quantity even when only changing status. Acceptable but not a true partial-PATCH; document this in OpenAPI or accept Optional[product]/Optional[quantity].
PATCH /api/inventory/outward does not call ensure_product on the (possibly renamed) product, unlike the inward PATCH. Minor inconsistency.

Defaults endpoint merges only two sections (inward, outward) — if future work adds e.g. 'audit' or 'transfer' the deep-merge will silently drop them. Consider {**existing, **{k:{...} for k in data.dict(...)}} pattern.
test_credentials: bulk-test@solarix.com / Test@1234 (primary). Secondary tenant freshly registered per session under test_sprint5_<uuid>@solarix-tests.com.
seed_data_creation: All test data prefixed TEST_S5_* (products, inward, outward, clients, material requests, secondary tenant TEST_Sprint5_Tenant_*). Each test cleans its own large fixtures where reasonable; some product/inward/outward TEST_S5_ rows remain after the run by design (harmless and useful for next iteration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 5 inventory backend is green (28/28). Primary tenant DB now has additional TEST_S5_* products / inward / outward rows + 1 approved material-request with a Dispatched auto-outward. Defaults doc for the primary tenant has been written with {inward:{unit:'Nos',source_type:'Supplier'}, outward:{unit:'Kg'}} — keep this in mind if your tests assert empty defaults. History endpoint is Python-side filtered (case-insensitive substring). Pending outward entries are correctly excluded from balance everywhere (stats + products list). Multi-tenant isolation has been verified at every Sprint 5 endpoint. Sprint 2 AI bulk-import is still wired and entries show source='ai-bulk-import' in /inventory/history.
